source_policy.repo_write_scope: Only this lane directory was written.
source_policy.protected_eval_labels: Not read. Measurements used manifests, mesh indexes, summaries, timing logs, and source code only.

measured_monoliths.label: older replay_native Burlington body_mesh.json, not this close-proof run
w5_p22latent Wolverine body_mesh.json, not this close-proof run

viewer_capability_today.display_fps_button: web/replay/src/App.tsx DisplayFpsControl labels the control '2x FPS (interpolated)'.
viewer_capability_today.world_skeleton_interpolation: web/replay/src/viewerData.ts displayFpsReplayData -> doubleFpsWorld inserts midpoint VirtualWorld frames by linearly interpolating joints_world, joint_conf, track_world_xy, transl_world, floor_world_xyz, and mesh_ref metadata.
viewer_capability_today.mesh_interpolation: web/replay/src/viewerData.ts doubleFpsBodyMesh inserts midpoint BodyMeshFrame only when the adjacent mesh frames are in the same source_window_index and have matching vertex/joint counts; interpolated mesh vertices and joints are linearly blended and marked mesh_interpolated=true with interpolation metadata.
viewer_capability_today.mesh_hold_without_2x: bodyMeshFrameForTime can hold a previous mesh over short same-run gaps before fading; this is hold behavior, not measured interpolation.
viewer_capability_today.current_bundle_limitation: The four close-proof mesh indexes have 100 selected mesh frames. Existing render-side interpolation cannot invent BODY evidence and is only cosmetic; it can blend available adjacent same-window vertices, but long inter-window gaps remain refused/held/blank depending on timing.
data_available_for_render_mesh_interp: interpolation metadata fields in viewer model
data_lacking_for_principled_interp: MHR latent pose-code sequence in the viewer bundle
validated latent round-trip/mesh-skeleton correspondence for interpolated frames

p22_tie_in.exists_today: P2-2 phase 1 landed an MHR decode wrapper and latent smoother prototype, but the smoother is unwired. w5_p22wiring produced only a marked world-joint proxy measurement for Wolverine.
p22_tie_in.measured_wiring_state: runs/lanes/w5_p22wiring_20260708/report.json reports PARTIAL: decoded smoothed candidate dirs absent; .venv lacked roma for local mhr_decode; process_video.py remained unwired.
p22_tie_in.what_it_could_enable_once_wired: If strict decoded latent round-trip and mesh-skeleton correspondence pass, replay could interpolate between BODY frames by smoothing/decoding latent pose codes so skeleton and mesh move coherently instead of simple render-side vertex blends.
p22_tie_in.not_available_today: No current close-proof viewer bundle carries MHR latents or decoded inter-frame BODY output; no lambda is wiring-ready from the available proxy report.

mechanism_table.measured_current_state: Viewer already has a 2x FPS display-only button. It interpolates world skeletons and eligible same-window mesh vertices; it marks interpolated mesh frames with mesh_interpolated=true/interpolation metadata. It does not create measured BODY evidence.

cost.engineering_scope: viewer-only labeling/banding and possibly looser same-topology mesh blend policy

mechanism_table.expected_playback_fps_gain: Display cadence can double for skeletons. Mesh gain is bounded by available adjacent mesh frames and same-window eligibility; long gaps such as Outdoor 483->593 remain non-measured cosmetic gaps unless the viewer explicitly blends across windows.
mechanism_table.never_measured_evidence_risk: High if hidden behind normal playback. Must label as display/cosmetic/interpolated, preserve original frame markers, and prevent export/scoring/training use.
mechanism_table.viewer_label: Display FPS: 2x interpolated, cosmetic only; measured frames remain tick-marked.

mechanism_table.measured_current_state: All four close-proof frame plans selected 100 world-mesh source frames. Effective unique mesh cadence is Burlington 9.99fps, Wolverine 10.00fps, Outdoor 5.21fps, IMG1605 10.10fps. Outdoor and IMG1605 have worst gaps around 1.8s.

cost.disk_transfer: Roughly linear in mesh-frame count plus one shared faces file. See per-clip cap_extrapolation.
cost.gpu_min: No extra BODY inference if it only exports more already-scheduled BODY frames; may add index build/download time.
cost.engineering_scope: pipeline/config cap change plus storage/viewer load checks

mechanism_table.expected_playback_fps_gain: Cap 200 roughly doubles mesh cadence where scheduled frames exist; cap 400/all reaches 20-65fps depending on clip schedule.
mechanism_table.never_measured_evidence_risk: Low. Frames remain measured BODY outputs, but still scoped/low-confidence BODY evidence, not promotion evidence.
mechanism_table.viewer_label: Measured BODY mesh frames, still scoped evidence; show cap/source count.

mechanism_table.measured_current_state: BODY scheduling already kept every source frame for Burlington, Wolverine, and Outdoor at the unique-frame level. IMG1605 has one BODY schedule gap frame 82->137 (55 frames). Scheduled player-frame counts are 1785, 680, 2653, and 359.

cost.gpu_min_estimate: Using measured steady H100 ms/person-frame only: Burlington 2x +0.45m, 4x +1.36m; Wolverine 2x +0.17m, 4x +0.52m; Outdoor 2x +0.67m, 4x +2.01m; IMG1605 2x +0.09m, 4x +0.28m. Full stage-wall scaling would be much larger because preprocessing/gate/index overhead dominates.
cost.engineering_scope: scheduler policy and remote BODY cost guard changes

mechanism_table.expected_playback_fps_gain: Only helps where BODY itself is sparse or where more player frames become eligible for mesh export. It does not fix a 100-frame mesh cap by itself.
mechanism_table.never_measured_evidence_risk: Low for emitted BODY frames; cost and quality drift risk are higher.
mechanism_table.viewer_label: Measured BODY frames; preserve schedule policy and cap provenance.

mechanism_table.measured_current_state: Prototype exists but is unwired. Available w5 wiring evidence is proxy-only and not wiring-ready; decoded candidate dirs/lambda coverage are absent locally.

cost.disk_transfer: TBD after latent payload format; likely smaller than dense vertices if latents are stored, but requires decode/runtime path.
cost.gpu_min: TBD; could avoid full SAM3D per-frame BODY only after validated decode/interp path.
cost.engineering_scope: raw latent persistence, strict GATE 1b, decoded candidate generation, viewer or pipeline decode wiring, trust-band UI

mechanism_table.expected_playback_fps_gain: Potentially fills between BODY frames with coherent mesh+skeleton once wired. No measured gain today.
mechanism_table.never_measured_evidence_risk: High unless inter-frame decoded output is visibly banded as latent-interpolated and excluded from metrics/training/promotion.
mechanism_table.viewer_label: Latent-interpolated BODY, cosmetic/derived; measured BODY frames separately tick-marked.

proposed_build_checklist_bullet: [W6 PLAYBACKDIAG 2026-07-08, Codex READ-ONLY] Measured close-proof 3D playback cadence and cost menu in runs/lanes/w6_playbackdiag_20260708/: all 4 close-proof runs selected 100 world-mesh source frames; effective mesh cadence is Burlington 9.99fps, Wolverine 10.00fps, Outdoor 5.21fps, IMG1605 10.10fps with worst mesh gaps 83/21/110/56 frames. Viewer 2x FPS is cosmetic render-side interpolation only; mesh cap raise is linear storage/transfer; denser scheduling has measured H100 steady cost but does not fix the cap alone; P2-2 latent interpolation remains unwired/proxy-only.
